greetings: Hey there, champion! Ready to crush today's goals? 🔥
Welcome back! Your future self is watching your every choice right now.
Look who showed up. Half the battle is just getting here. Let's GO.
The gym doesn't care about your mood. Neither do your gains. Let's work!
Another day, another chance to become absolutely unstoppable.

lazy: That's exactly why today matters. Future-you is already judging your excuses.
Laziness is just fear wearing sweatpants. Change into gym clothes instead.
The couch will always be there. Your youth won't. Move it!
Your muscles literally cannot grow if you're horizontal on the couch. Science said so.
Every champion you admire had days they didn't feel like it. They showed up anyway.
tired: Tired or lazy? Big difference. Tired still means we work — just smarter today.
Rest is earned, not given on day 1. Keep moving, then earn your sleep tonight.
Your body is 40% more capable than your brain admits when you're tired. Trust the process.
A 20-minute workout when tired does more than zero. Start with 5 minutes.
motivated: YES! Channel that energy RIGHT NOW before it disappears. Go lift something heavy.
This energy? BOTTLE IT. Use it today and remember how it feels when things get hard.
That's the spirit! Now let's build a plan worthy of that motivation.
skip: Missing one workout is fine. Missing two is a pattern. Missing three is a lifestyle choice.
Skipped yesterday? Today is your reset button. Use it.
Your muscles don't know what day it is. But they do know when you stopped showing up.

how_often_train.question: How many times should I work out per week?
how_often_train.answer: For beginners: 3x per week full body. Intermediate: 4x with push/pull splits. Advanced: 5-6x with specific muscle group splits. Rest is when you actually GROW — don't skip it.

how_long_results.question: How long until I see results?
how_long_results.answer: You'll FEEL results in 2 weeks. See results in 4-6 weeks. Others will notice in 8-12 weeks. Stay consistent — results are guaranteed if you show up.

cardio_or_weights.question: Should I do cardio or weights first?
cardio_or_weights.answer: If your goal is muscle gain: weights first, cardio second (or separate sessions). If fat loss: either works, but weights builds the metabolic engine that keeps burning after the gym.

protein_timing.question: When should I eat protein?
protein_timing.answer: The 'anabolic window' isn't as tight as bro science claims. Spread protein across 4-5 meals throughout the day. Post-workout protein within 2 hours is still smart though.

plateau.question: I've hit a plateau. What do I do?
plateau.answer: 1) Add progressive overload — more weight or reps. 2) Change rep ranges. 3) Check sleep and nutrition. 4) Deload week. 5) New exercises. Plateau = your body adapted. Shock it.

sore_muscles.question: My muscles are really sore. Should I still train?
sore_muscles.answer: Light soreness? Train through it — blood flow helps recovery. Severe soreness or pain? Rest or do active recovery (walks, stretching). Pain is feedback. Listen to it.

membership.question: What does FITVERSE membership include?
membership.answer: Your FITVERSE membership unlocks: unlimited AI coaching, access to 500+ workout programs, custom meal plans, community challenges, progress tracking, and live expert sessions. Plans start at $9.99/month.

quotes: The body achieves what the mind believes.
No pain, no gain — but also no brain, no gain.
You don't have to be extreme. Just consistent.
The only bad workout is the one that didn't happen.
Your body hears everything your mind says. Say the right things.
Fall seven times, get up eight. Then do eight more reps.
Motivation gets you started. Discipline keeps you going.
Be stronger than your strongest excuse.
The mirror doesn't lie — but it takes time to tell the truth.
Train insane or remain the same.
Fitness is not about being better than someone else — it's about being better than you used to be.
Results happen over time, not overnight. Work hard, stay consistent, and be patient.
Your only competition is the person you were yesterday.
